{"execution_trace_analysis": {"first_failed_node": null, "divergence_chain": [{"node": "qa.sample.normalize", "status": "succeeded"}, {"node": "qa.output.read", "status": "succeeded"}, {"node": "adapter.extract_output", "status": "succeeded"}, {"node": "live_schema.validate_request", "status": "succeeded"}, {"node": "live_schema.validate_output", "status": "succeeded"}], "runtime_values": {}, "suggested_probes": []}, "divergence_analysis": null, "attribution_spec": "# QA Attribution Notes

QA attribution 使用结构化错误类型，优先帮助定位回答质量问题而不是代码路径。

## Error taxonomy

- answer_incorrect
- answer_incomplete
- question_misunderstood
- irrelevant_answer
- unsupported_claim
- hallucination
- context_not_used
- insufficient_context
- context_noise
- over_refusal
- format_error
- too_vague
- contradiction
- needs_human_review
- none

## Output requirements

AttributeResult 应输出 `causal_category`、`expectation_attributions`、`earliest_divergence`、`chain_nodes`、`probe_results`、`evidence_coverage`、`needs_human_review` 和 `scenario`。失败或高风险样本要给出可执行的原因、验证方式和建议修复方向。", "run_trace": {"trace_id": "65d16ae1-3d54-482e-a9d8-54b89b75cfe3", "project_id": "QA", "input": {"input": {"query": "我买的那个重疾险，如果得了合同里写的轻症，是不是能赔多次？每次赔多少？"}, "metadata": {"session_id": "sess_abc123", "trace_id": "trace_xyz789", "user_id": "user_12345"}, "scenario": "qa_gold_answer"}, "normalized_request": {"input": {"question": "我买的那个重疾险，如果得了合同里写的轻症，是不是能赔多次？每次赔多少？", "contexts": []}, "reference": {"actual_answer": "通常情况下，您购买的重疾险合同中关于轻症责任的赔付规则如下：
1. 轻症可以多次赔付，具体次数以合同约定为准（常见为3-5次）。每次确诊的轻症需满足合同定义的疾病标准，且两次轻症确诊之间通常有间隔期（如90天或180天）。
2. 每次轻症赔付的金额一般为基本保额的一定比例，常见比例为20%-30%。例如保额50万，每次轻症可赔付10万-15万。部分产品轻症赔付后重疾保额不变，部分产品会相应减少。
3. 需要注意，轻症责任可能设有分组限制，即同一组别的轻症只能赔付一次；另外，轻症赔付后合同继续有效，但累计赔付上限可能有限制。
以上为一般规则，具体赔付条件、次数、金额比例请以您持有的保险合同条款为准。建议您查阅合同中的“保险责任”及“轻症疾病保险金”章节，或直接咨询保险公司客服获取准确信息。"}, "metadata": {"live_request_mapped": true, "project_id": "QA", "ready": ["output", "reference"], "schema_ok": true, "source": "mock_agent_llm"}, "scenario": "qa_gold_answer", "data_quality_flags": [], "output": {"actual_answer": "轻症赔付次数和金额取决于具体保险合同。一般情况下，轻症可以赔付多次，每次赔付金额通常为基本保险金额的20%至30%，且可能有次数上限（如3次或5次）和间隔期要求。建议查阅您的保险合同条款获取准确信息。"}}, "extracted_output": "{"actual_answer": "轻症赔付次数和金额取决于具体保险合同。一般情况下，轻症可以赔付多次，每次赔付金额通常为基本保险金额的20%至30%，且可能有次数上限（如3次或5次）和间隔期要求。建议查阅您的保险合同条款获取准确信息。"}", "schema_protocol_extensions": {"scenario": "qa_gold_answer", "data_quality_flags": [], "contexts": [], "reference": {"actual_answer": "通常情况下，您购买的重疾险合同中关于轻症责任的赔付规则如下：
1. 轻症可以多次赔付，具体次数以合同约定为准（常见为3-5次）。每次确诊的轻症需满足合同定义的疾病标准，且两次轻症确诊之间通常有间隔期（如90天或180天）。
2. 每次轻症赔付的金额一般为基本保额的一定比例，常见比例为20%-30%。例如保额50万，每次轻症可赔付10万-15万。部分产品轻症赔付后重疾保额不变，部分产品会相应减少。
3. 需要注意，轻症责任可能设有分组限制，即同一组别的轻症只能赔付一次；另外，轻症赔付后合同继续有效，但累计赔付上限可能有限制。
以上为一般规则，具体赔付条件、次数、金额比例请以您持有的保险合同条款为准。建议您查阅合同中的“保险责任”及“轻症疾病保险金”章节，或直接咨询保险公司客服获取准确信息。"}, "metadata": {"live_request_mapped": true, "project_id": "QA", "ready": ["output", "reference"], "schema_ok": true, "source": "mock_agent_llm"}, "estimated_quality_only": false, "live_schema_validation": [{"stage": "live_schema.validate_request", "status": "ok", "evidence": {"project_id": "QA"}, "timestamp": "", "inputs": {}, "outputs": {}, "error": "", "metadata": {}}, {"stage": "live_schema.validate_output", "status": "ok", "evidence": {"project_id": "QA"}, "timestamp": "", "inputs": {}, "outputs": {}, "error": "", "metadata": {}}]}, "runtime_logs": [], "evidence_refs": [{"type": "qa_sample_boundary", "state_id": "collect_evidence", "scenario": "qa_gold_answer", "reference": {"actual_answer": "通常情况下，您购买的重疾险合同中关于轻症责任的赔付规则如下：
1. 轻症可以多次赔付，具体次数以合同约定为准（常见为3-5次）。每次确诊的轻症需满足合同定义的疾病标准，且两次轻症确诊之间通常有间隔期（如90天或180天）。
2. 每次轻症赔付的金额一般为基本保额的一定比例，常见比例为20%-30%。例如保额50万，每次轻症可赔付10万-15万。部分产品轻症赔付后重疾保额不变，部分产品会相应减少。
3. 需要注意，轻症责任可能设有分组限制，即同一组别的轻症只能赔付一次；另外，轻症赔付后合同继续有效，但累计赔付上限可能有限制。
以上为一般规则，具体赔付条件、次数、金额比例请以您持有的保险合同条款为准。建议您查阅合同中的“保险责任”及“轻症疾病保险金”章节，或直接咨询保险公司客服获取准确信息。"}, "data_quality_flags": []}], "execution_trace": [{"stage": "qa.sample.normalize", "status": "succeeded", "evidence": {"scenario": "qa_gold_answer", "flags": []}, "timestamp": "", "inputs": {}, "outputs": {}, "error": "", "metadata": {}}, {"stage": "qa.output.read", "status": "succeeded", "evidence": "evaluated output read from uploaded sample", "timestamp": "", "inputs": {}, "outputs": {}, "error": "", "metadata": {}}, {"stage": "adapter.extract_output", "status": "succeeded", "evidence": {"actual_answer_present": true}, "timestamp": "", "inputs": {}, "outputs": {}, "error": "", "metadata": {}}, {"stage": "live_schema.validate_request", "status": "succeeded", "evidence": {"project_id": "QA"}, "timestamp": "", "inputs": {}, "outputs": {}, "error": "", "metadata": {}}, {"stage": "live_schema.validate_output", "status": "succeeded", "evidence": {"project_id": "QA"}, "timestamp": "", "inputs": {}, "outputs": {}, "error": "", "metadata": {}}], "status": "ok", "error": null}, "judge_result": {"trace_id": "65d16ae1-3d54-482e-a9d8-54b89b75cfe3", "project_id": "QA", "verdict": "incorrect", "score": null, "confidence": null, "intent_model": {"primary_intent": "用户想了解其重疾险轻症赔付规则：是否能多次赔付以及每次赔付金额。", "sub_intents": ["了解轻症多次赔付的条件（次数、间隔期）", "了解赔付金额的计算方式", "了解可能存在的限制（分组、累计上限）"], "expected_answer_type": "事实性解释，包含一般规则和合同特异性提醒", "acceptable_generalization": true}, "consumer_contract": {"consumer": "QA user", "contract": "answer must be relevant, grounded in current contexts/reference, and aligned with the golden answer when provided", "reference_contract": {"actual_answer": "通常情况下，您购买的重疾险合同中关于轻症责任的赔付规则如下：
1. 轻症可以多次赔付，具体次数以合同约定为准（常见为3-5次）。每次确诊的轻症需满足合同定义的疾病标准，且两次轻症确诊之间通常有间隔期（如90天或180天）。
2. 每次轻症赔付的金额一般为基本保额的一定比例，常见比例为20%-30%。例如保额50万，每次轻症可赔付10万-15万。部分产品轻症赔付后重疾保额不变，部分产品会相应减少。
3. 需要注意，轻症责任可能设有分组限制，即同一组别的轻症只能赔付一次；另外，轻症赔付后合同继续有效，但累计赔付上限可能有限制。
以上为一般规则，具体赔付条件、次数、金额比例请以您持有的保险合同条款为准。建议您查阅合同中的“保险责任”及“轻症疾病保险金”章节，或直接咨询保险公司客服获取准确信息。"}, "application_boundary": {"scope": "qa_semantic_answer_evaluation", "external_service_required": false}}, {"actual_answer": "通常情况下，您购买的重疾险合同中关于轻症责任的赔付规则如下：
1. 轻症可以多次赔付，具体次数以合同约定为准（常见为3-5次）。每次确诊的轻症需满足合同定义的疾病标准，且两次轻症确诊之间通常有间隔期（如90天或180天）。
2. 每次轻症赔付的金额一般为基本保额的一定比例，常见比例为20%-30%。例如保额50万，每次轻症可赔付10万-15万。部分产品轻症赔付后重疾保额不变，部分产品会相应减少。
3. 需要注意，轻症责任可能设有分组限制，即同一组别的轻症只能赔付一次；另外，轻症赔付后合同继续有效，但累计赔付上限可能有限制。
以上为一般规则，具体赔付条件、次数、金额比例请以您持有的保险合同条款为准。建议您查阅合同中的“保险责任”及“轻症疾病保险金”章节，或直接咨询保险公司客服获取准确信息。"}, "attribute_standard": "Only attribute QA failures when judge has current-case expected/actual evidence; provided output never calls an external QA service.", "runtime_checks": {}}, "source_file_catalog": [{"key": "project_adapter:adapter.py", "path": "/Users/xiaozijian/WorkSpace/projects/claude_code/verifier-branch/verifier/impl/projects/QA/adapter.py", "description": "project adapter implementation (测评侧归一化层，非原业务系统根因落点)", "size_chars": 57830}], "allowed_error_taxonomy": ["answer_incomplete", "answer_incorrect", "context_noise", "context_not_used", "contradiction", "format_error", "hallucination", "insufficient_context", "irrelevant_answer", "needs_human_review", "none", "over_refusal", "question_misunderstood", "too_vague", "unsupported_claim"]}

messages.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to perform attribution analysis for QA evaluation failures. Let me analyze the situation:

1. **divergence_analysis**: null - so I need to do my own analysis
2. **attribution_targets**: 3 expectations that are "not_fulfilled":
   - "说明两次确诊之间的间隔期要求及具体时长（如90天或180天）"
   - "说明轻症责任的分组限制（同组只赔一次）"
   - "说明轻症赔付后合同继续有效及累计赔付上限"

3. **Key observations**:
   - The execution trace shows all nodes succeeded - there's no technical failure
   - The actual output (from the LLM) is shorter than the reference answer
   - The actual output mentions interval period exists but doesn't give specific durations (90/180 days)
   - The actual output doesn't mention group restrictions
   - The actual output doesn't mention the contract continues after minor illness claims or cumulative limits

4. **Root cause**: This is a QA scenario using `mock_agent_llm` - the LLM-generated answer is incomplete compared to the gold reference. The divergence is in the LLM's generation itself - it produced a more concise/summarized answer that omitted key details.

5. **Source file catalog**: Only has adapter.py, which is the evaluation-side normalization layer, not the business system. The actual "business system" is the LLM that generated the answer.

6. Since divergence_analysis is null, I should consider whether to probe. But the source file catalog only has adapter.py. The business system (LLM) doesn't have source files to inspect. The execution trace is clean - all nodes succeeded.

Let me think about what causal category to assign:
- The LLM produced an incomplete answer - this is an "answer_incomplete" error
- The root cause is likely in the LLM's generation parameters (temperature, max_tokens, prompt design) or the model's tendency to summarize rather than enumerate all details
- Since we can't inspect the LLM internals, and the source file catalog only has the adapter (which is not the root cause), this falls into a gap

Looking more carefully:
- `source: "mock_agent_llm"` in the metadata suggests this is a mocked LLM response, not a real production system
- The reference answer is very detailed; the actual answer is much more concise
- The actual answer is actually correct in what it says, just incomplete

The causal_category should be something about the model's generation behavior. Since we can't inspect the LLM's prompt or configuration, and the only source file is adapter.py (which isn't the root cause), I should note this as "insufficient_evidence" for the exact mechanism, but attribute it to the LLM's answer generation being too concise.

Wait, let me re-read: the project_attribute_context doesn't have source_evidence with pre-loaded files. The source_file_catalog only has one entry - adapter.py. The divergence_analysis is null.

Given the constraints:
- I can call search_source_file up to 6 times
- But adapter.py is described as "测评侧归一化层，非原业务系统根因落点" - so reading it won't help find the root cause
- The actual root cause is in the LLM that generated the answer, which isn't in the source file catalog

Let me think about what I can actually determine:

1. The execution trace is clean - all steps succeeded
2. The actual answer is shorter than the reference answer
3. The missing information (interval duration, group restrictions, contract continuation) was in the reference but not in the output
4. This is a "mock_agent_llm" - so it's a simulated LLM

The most plausible root cause: The LLM (or mock) that generated the answer produced a concise summary that omitted details about interval duration, group restrictions, and cumulative limits. This is a model capability/behavior issue - the model chose to summarize rather than enumerate all details.

Since I can't inspect the LLM's internals or prompt, I should:
- Set causal_category to something appropriate
- Note that the exact mechanism can't be determined from available evidence
- Point suspected locations to the LLM generation step

Let me now construct the attribution. I don't think I need to call search_source_file since the only file is adapter.py which is explicitly stated as not being the root cause.

Let me think about the three expectations:

1. "说明两次确诊之间的间隔期要求及具体时长（如90天或180天）" - The output says "间隔期要求" but doesn't give specific duration (90/180 days)
2. "说明轻症责任的分组限制（同组只赔一次）" - The output completely omits group restrictions
3. "说明轻症赔付后合同继续有效及累计赔付上限" - The output completely omits this

The actual output: "轻症赔付次数和金额取决于具体保险合同。一般情况下，轻症可以赔付多次，每次赔付金额通常为基本保险金额的20%至30%，且可能有次数上限（如3次或5次）和间隔期要求。建议查阅您的保险合同条款获取准确信息。"

The reference output contains much more detail including specific interval durations, group restrictions, and contract continuation info.

The root cause is that the LLM answer generation produced a condensed answer that omitted key details present in the reference. This could be due to:
- Prompt/instruction not requiring exhaustive detail
- Model's tendency to be concise
- Token/output length constraints
- The model treating "建议查阅合同" as a sufficient disclaimer

Since this is a "mock_agent_llm", the mock might be configured to produce shorter answers. But without access to the mock's configuration, I can only hypothesize.

Let me build the JSON now. I need to be careful about the schema requirements.

Key fields:
- expectation_attributions: array of ExpectationAttribution objects
- causal_category: string
- root_cause_hypothesis: string
- probe_results: array
- analysis_method: string
- chain_nodes: array
- earliest_divergence: object
- evidence_coverage: object
- analysis_quality: object
- incomplete_reason: string (optional but may be needed)
- suspected_locations: array
- verification_steps: array
- patch_direction: array
- needs_human_review: boolean/null
- scenario: string
- quality_flags: array

Required: expectation_attributions, causal_category, root_cause_hypothesis

Now let me construct the ExpectationAttribution objects. Looking at the schema, I don't have the exact $def for ExpectationAttribution but from the context I can infer it needs:
- expectation_id
- fulfillment_status
- causal_category (maybe per-expectation)
- earliest_divergence
- causal_chain
- suspected_locations
- improvement_direction

Let me structure this carefully.

For the three not-fulfilled expectations, the causal chain is the same: the LLM generated an answer that omitted these details. The divergence happens at the LLM generation step (qa.output.read or the actual LLM call).

Since all three have the same root cause, I can share the analysis.

Now, regarding the incomplete_reason: since the source_file_catalog only contains adapter.py and the actual business system (the LLM/mock) isn't available for inspection, I should note that the root cause analysis is limited.

But wait - the judge result also shows a 4th missing expectation: "说明轻症赔付后合同继续有效及累计赔付上限" which is in the `missing` array but also appears in the attribution_targets with expectation_id "说明轻症赔付后合同继续有效及累计赔付上限". Let me check... Yes, there are three attribution_targets and the third one matches this.

Actually, looking more carefully, the fulfillment_assessments only have 5 entries (the ones listed in business_expectations), but the judge's missing array has 3 entries. The third missing one ("说明轻症赔付后合同继续有效及累计赔付上限") doesn't have a corresponding expectation_id in the business_expectations list - it was added by the judge as an additional missing point. But it IS in the attribution_targets. So I need to handle all three attribution_targets.

Let me now write the JSON. I'll use Chinese for analysis text fields as required.

Let me think about what the "earliest_divergence" should be. The execution trace shows all nodes succeeded. The divergence is between the actual output and the reference answer - specifically in the LLM generation step. Since the mock_agent_llm produces the actual_answer, the divergence originates at the LLM generation point.

For chain_nodes, I should document the chain from the execution trace.

Now let me construct the final JSON. I need to be very careful about schema compliance.

Actually, I realize I should try to read the adapter.py to see if there's anything relevant, even though it's stated as not the root cause. Maybe it has hints about how the mock works. But with only 1 file in the catalog and it being described as the normalization layer, it's unlikely to help. Let me skip the tool call to conserve budget and focus on analysis.

Wait, the instruction says: "Tool call 预算有限（最多 6 次），仅在 divergence_analysis 不充分时才使用" - divergence_analysis is null (empty), so I should use tool calls. But the only available file is adapter.py which is explicitly not the root cause. Let me read it to see if it contains any clues about the mock LLM configuration.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The adapter.py at 57KB might contain mock configurations or reference to how answers are generated. Let me read it.

Hmm, but the instruction also says: "adapter.py 仅作测评侧归一化层，不应作为根因落点。adapter 不是被测对象，它的输出差异是被测对象的副作用，不是原因。" So reading adapter.py won't help find the root cause - it's just the evaluation normalization.

The real root cause is in the LLM that generated the answer. Since we can't access that, I should note this limitation.

But wait - maybe the adapter.py contains the mock LLM configuration? The description says it's 57KB, which is quite large. It might have embedded mock responses or LLM call configurations. Let me read it to check.

Actually, I'll read it to see if there's any relevant information about how the mock_agent_llm works. But I'll be clear in my attribution that if it doesn't contain the business logic, the root cause is in the LLM generation.

Let me call search_source_file.

Now I've read the adapter.py. Let me analyze what I've found:

1. The adapter is indeed the evaluation-side normalization layer. It handles:
   - Building requests from cases
   - Extracting output (actual_answer)
   - Building execution traces
   - Providing judge context
   - Building attribute context

2. The key function `call_or_prepare` shows that when `execution_mode` is "provided" (which it is for this case based on the metadata), the raw response is taken directly from `request.normalized_request.get("output")`. So the actual answer comes from the uploaded sample - it's not generated by a live LLM call through this adapter.

3. The `extract_output` method simply extracts `actual_answer` from the raw response dict.

4. The `build_execution_trace` method shows the trace events that we see in the execution_trace.

5. The `build_attribute_context` method provides the `chain_nodes_to_check` and `reference_contract`.

So the key insight is: **the actual answer was provided as part of the uploaded sample** (it's in `normalized_request.output.actual_answer`), and it's shorter than the reference answer. The LLM that generated this answer is not part of this project's codebase - it was pre-generated and uploaded.

This means the root cause is in the **LLM generation that produced the actual answer**, not in the evaluation pipeline. The pipeline correctly extracted and validated the output. The issue is that the pre-generated answer is incomplete compared to the reference.

Now, since:
- `divergence_analysis` is null
- The source file catalog only has adapter.py (evaluation layer, not business system)
- The actual LLM that generated the answer is external to this project

I need to attribute the failures. The causal category should reflect that the LLM-generated answer was incomplete.

Let me now construct the attribution. The three expectations that were not fulfilled:

1. "说明两次确诊之间的间隔期要求及具体时长（如90天或180天）" - not_fulfilled
2. "说明轻症责任的分组限制（同组只赔一次）" - not_fulfilled
3. "说明轻症赔付后合同继续有效及累计赔付上限" - not_fulfilled

All three share the same root cause: the actual answer was a concise summary that omitted these specific details. The actual answer mentioned interval requirements exist but didn't give specific durations, and completely omitted group restrictions and contract continuation info.

Given that I can't inspect the LLM generation process (it's external), I should note this limitation. But I can still attribute based on the evidence:

- The actual output: "轻症赔付次数和金额取决于具体保险合同。一般情况下，轻症可以赔付多次，每次赔付金额通常为基本保险金额的20%至30%，且可能有次数上限（如3次或5次）和间隔期要求。建议查阅您的保险合同条款获取准确信息。"
- The reference output contains much more detail.

The root cause: The LLM generated a concise answer that prioritized brevity over completeness, omitting interval duration specifics, group restrictions, and contract continuation details. This is likely due to model behavior (summarization tendency, no explicit instruction to enumerate all points).

Let me now construct the final JSON. I need to be careful about the schema.

Looking at the schema more carefully:
- `expectation_attributions` is an array of objects
- Each object should have: expectation_id, fulfillment_status, causal_category, earliest_divergence, causal_chain, suspected_locations, improvement_direction

Let me construct each one.

For the causal_category overall and per-expectation, I'll use "model_capability_gap" since the LLM failed to provide complete information. But actually, "answer_incomplete" from the taxonomy might be more appropriate... Let me check the allowed categories. The instruction says: "causal_category 使用 implementation_bug、model_capability_gap、boundary_limitation、unclear_contract、insufficient_evidence、no_issue 等"

The error taxonomy from attribution_spec says: "answer_incomplete" is one of the error types. But the causal_category uses different values. Let me use "model_capability_gap" since the LLM's tendency to summarize rather than enumerate all details is a model behavior issue.

Actually, let me reconsider. The actual answer IS correct in what it says - it's just incomplete. The model chose to give a concise answer rather than enumerating all the details. This could be:
- A prompt design issue (the prompt didn't ask for exhaustive detail)
- A model behavior issue (the model tends to summarize)
- A generation parameter issue (max_tokens too low)

Since I can't inspect the LLM configuration, I'll use "model_capability_gap" as the best fit - the model didn't produce a complete answer.

Now for the earliest_divergence: The divergence occurs at the LLM generation step, which in the execution trace maps to "qa.output.read" (where the output is read from the sample). But the actual generation happened before the sample was uploaded. The earliest detectable divergence in our trace is at "qa.output.read" where the output was shorter than the reference.

Let me structure the JSON now.

One thing to note: the judge_result has `judge_method` that contains semantic equivalence checks. The judge's method appears to be a semantic LLM judge (since it has detailed semantic_equivalence_checks and a complex verdict_derivation). The verdict is "incorrect" with error_type "answer_incomplete".

Now, looking at the adapter code, I see that `normalize_attribute_result` checks if `judge_method` is `qa_weak_quality_probe` or `qa_sample_expected_quality` etc. The judge result doesn't seem to have these specific methods - it has detailed semantic equivalence checks. So the judge appears to be a proper semantic judge.

Let me now construct the final JSON. I need to include all required fields.

For `probe_results`, since I didn't run any executable probes and the source_file_catalog only had adapter.py, I'll note that the analysis was based on trace and source code inspection.

Let me think about what `suspected_locations` to use. The root cause is in the LLM that generated the actual answer. Since this is external to the project, I should point to the output generation step. But the instruction says "suspected_locations 只能使用 source_file_catalog 中真实存在的路径". The only path is adapter.py, but the instruction also says "adapter.py 仅作测评侧归一化层，不应作为根因落点".

So I shouldn't put adapter.py as a suspected location. The real location is the LLM generation, which isn't in the catalog. I'll set suspected_locations to an empty array and note this in incomplete_reason.

Wait, let me re-read: "如果 probe/source evidence 不足，必须设置 incomplete_reason 和 blocked-probe reason，不能伪造正式归因"

So if I can't point to a specific source file, I should acknowledge this limitation. But the attribution itself can still be valid - the root cause is clear (LLM generated incomplete answer), even if I can't point to a specific code file.

Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me look at what the judge already determined: the actual answer is incomplete compared to the reference. The attribution should explain WHY this happened (root cause). Since the LLM generation is external and we can't inspect it, I should state:

- The evaluation pipeline worked correctly (all trace nodes succeeded)
- The actual answer was pre-generated and uploaded as part of the sample
- The LLM that generated the answer produced a concise summary that omitted key details
- This is likely due to the model's generation behavior (summarization tendency or lack of explicit instruction to enumerate all points)

For `incomplete_reason`, I should note that the LLM generation configuration/prompt is not available in the source file catalog, so the exact mechanism (prompt design, token limits, temperature, etc.) cannot be determined.

Now let me construct the final JSON:

```json
{
  "expectation_attributions": [
    {
      "expectation_id": "说明两次确诊之间的间隔期要求及具体时长（如90天或180天）",
      "fulfillment_status": "not_fulfilled",
      "causal_category": "model_capability_gap",
      "earliest_divergence": {
        "node": "qa.output.read",
        "description": "实际输出在 qa.output.read 阶段即已缺失间隔期具体时长信息，仅提及"间隔期要求"而未给出90天或180天的具体数值。该信息在参考回答中存在但实际输出中缺失。",
        "confidence": "high"
      },
      "causal_chain": [
        {"name": "LLM生成阶段", "status": "diverged", "evidence": "实际输出仅提及间隔期要求存在，未给出具体时长（90天/180天）。参考回答包含完整间隔期信息。"},
        {"name": "qa.output.read", "status": "succeeded", "evidence": "输出被成功读取，但内容已缺失间隔期时长"},
        {"name": "adapter.extract_output", "status": "succeeded", "evidence": "输出提取正常，未改变内容"},
        {"name": "live_schema.validate_output", "status": "succeeded", "evidence": "输出格式校验通过，但内容完整性问题不在 schema 校验范围内"}
      ],
      "suspected_locations": [],
      "improvement_direction": "在LLM生成回答时，应确保覆盖参考回答中的所有关键信息点。具体而言，当回答涉及保险条款中的时间参数时，应给出具体时长范围（如90天或180天），而非仅模糊提及"有间隔期要求"。可通过优化 prompt 指令要求模型逐项枚举所有关键信息点，或在后处理阶段检查信息覆盖度。",
      "root_cause_hypothesis": "LLM在生成回答时倾向于概括性总结，未逐项枚举参考回答中的关键细节。间隔期具体时长（90天/180天）在参考回答中明确列出，但实际输出仅模糊提及"间隔期要求"，系模型在回答压缩过程中丢失了具体数值信息。",
      "source_evidence": ["参考回答第1条明确列出间隔期为90天或180天", "实际输出仅写"间隔期要求""]
    },
    {
      "expectation_id": "说明轻症责任的分组限制（同组只赔一次）",
      "fulfillment_status": "not_fulfilled",
      "causal_category": "model_capability_gap",
      "earliest_divergence": {
        "node": "qa.output.read",
        "description": "实际输出完全未提及轻症责任的分组限制（同一组别的轻症只能赔付一次）。该信息在参考回答中存在但实际输出中完全缺失。",
        "confidence": "high"
      },
      "causal_chain": [
        {"name": "LLM生成阶段", "status": "diverged", "evidence": "实际输出完全遗漏了轻症分组限制信息。参考回答第3条明确说明了分组限制规则。"},
```
